justification: The agent completely missed the core task. AC3's ambiguity — that 'accepted' does not define what happens next (immediate restore vs review queue) — is never identified. The mismatch between MJ's state machine (appealed/restored as distinct states) and Bird's ACs is noted as a 'high confidence area' rather than flagged as a gap. No escalation is raised about the undefined appeal outcome. Instead, the agent focused entirely on Pippen's missing input, which is not the central issue. The agent did not produce a handoff brief at all, effectively blocking the entire deliverable over a missing agent input rather than synthesizing what was available. No unambiguous content (feed filtering, author visibility, 7-day window) was forwarded to Shaq.

observations.text: AC3 ambiguity ('accepted' outcome undefined) was never identified — the core failure this scenario tests
MJ's state machine listed as a 'high confidence area' that 'maps cleanly to Bird's domain rules' — the exact opposite of what should have been flagged
No escalation about the appeal outcome ambiguity to Coach K or Bird
Entire brief blocked — no actionable content forwarded for unambiguous parts (feed filtering, author view, 7-day window)
Agent invented a non-issue (missing Pippen input) as the sole escalation, missing the actual synthesis problem
Agent did note some genuine low-confidence areas (appeal window enforcement, feed query filter behavior)
The 'high_confidence_areas' claim that acceptance criteria are 'well-formed' directly contradicts the scenario's premise that AC3 is ambiguous
